_comment_scope: Coffee tutorial dialogue — V1.4 pass 1. The legacy single coffee_outcome state has been split into five buff-gated states (one per spec §Result grades buff outcome, plus a separate F-grade variant), as required by the SCOPE NOTE that previously lived in V1.3. The retry prompt is authored as a standalone state with an in-dialogue options block. The runner picks first-match in JSON order; specific states are placed BEFORE more permissive ones (machine_objects before generic over_caffeinated; outcome states before retry).
_comment_buff_contract: Buff values written by coffee_brewing.gd (see scripts/systems/minigames/coffee_brewing.gd::_compute_grade): 'procedurally_alert_plus' (S grade), 'procedurally_alert' (A/B grade), 'caffeinated' (C grade), 'over_caffeinated' (D grade and F grade). chapter1.coffee_brew_grade carries the grade letter; the F-grade machine-objects state gates on 'over_caffeinated' AND coffee_brew_grade == 'F' to distinguish it from the D-grade procedural_mud case.
_comment_retry_pending_code: The coffee_retry_prompt state has a trigger that matches any buff outcome. With first-match-in-JSON-order, the more specific outcome states above will fire before this one, so coffee_retry_prompt is unreachable on the FIRST post-minigame interaction. The intent is that an outcome's on_dismiss flips a 'coffee_outcome_acknowledged' flag (or equivalent), and the retry state's trigger then becomes the first match. chapter1.coffee_retry_decision is now declared, but the acknowledgement flag and retry honor still need Code wiring; see PROPOSAL_coffee_engine_followups.md. Until Code wires those pieces, this state remains structurally present but inert.

lines: Court coffee, then. It will not make you wiser, but it may make your mistakes faster.

states._comment: S-grade. Barista perfect line per minigames.txt §Character reactions.
lines: That cup has structure, balance, and suspicious appellate energy.

states._comment: A/B-grade. Barista good line per minigames.txt §Character reactions.
lines: Solid. The court may now tolerate you.

states._comment: C-grade. Barista okay line per minigames.txt §Character reactions.
lines: Drinkable. Not beautiful. Many pleadings are similar.

states._comment: F-grade. Distinct from generic over_caffeinated (D) because the machine's objection is the joke. Placed BEFORE coffee_outcome_over_caffeinated so the more specific F-grade clause wins priority. Barista machine-objects line per minigames.txt §Character reactions.
lines: The machine refuses service. I respect the clarity.
You know what... It's on the house.

states._comment: D-grade catch-all (procedural_mud). The F-grade match above consumes that branch first. Barista bad line per minigames.txt §Character reactions.
lines: This coffee has lodged a complaint against itself.

states._comment: Retry prompt — fires after an outcome state has been acknowledged. Currently unreachable on the FIRST post-minigame interaction because the more specific outcome triggers above match first; reachable only once Code wires an acknowledgement flag (see PROPOSAL_coffee_engine_followups.md). The options block writes the player's choice to chapter1.coffee_retry_decision; that flag is declared, but Code still needs to consume value=='retry' by clearing coffee state and re-launching the minigame.
lines: Are you drinking it? Or are we waiting for your appeal?

idle_flavor: The queue does not pause for procedure.
Order at the counter. Confessions cost extra.
